personal.name: Lavanya Kotte
personal.title: AI/ML Engineer | Software Developer | Cloud Advocate

professionalSummary.text: Dedicated and ambitious undergraduate specializing in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning with hands-on expertise in machine learning workflows, deep learning architectures, and cloud databases. Proven ability in developing end-to-end full-stack applications, Flutter mobile tools, and complex workflow automations. Published research author with strong leadership capability, seeking to contribute technical skills to state-of-the-art software systems and AI solutions.

education.degree: B.Tech in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning

projects.title: Student Attendance Tracker Automation
projects.description: Manual student attendance logging is time-consuming and error-prone, resulting in delayed notifications to students/parents and suboptimal academic records administration.
projects.contribution: Designed and developed the complete frontend and backend, created database models, wrote REST APIs, and configured n8n visual flow rules and Twilio alerting triggers.
achievements: Interactive dashboard for viewing, recording, and reporting real-time attendance.
Custom n8n workflow engine executing seamless record synchronizations.
Automated Twilio SMS triggers dispatching alert notifications to students instantly.
Robust database integration using Supabase and PostgreSQL with secure user role authorization.

projects.title: QuickBite – Smart Food Ordering System
projects.description: Traditional dining processes suffer from slow ordering speeds, order customization errors, and a lack of real-time customer tracking interfaces.
projects.contribution: Architecting backend database schemas, building REST API endpoints, and constructing responsive mobile screens.
achievements: Cross-platform Flutter application for fluid customer interactions.
Node.js Express backend serving dynamic menu items and tracking requests.
Highly customizable customer orders with persistent historical preferences.

projects.title: FaithAI – AI-Powered Chatbot
projects.description: Users seeking theological or faith-based guidance frequently encounter impersonal, generic chatbot responses lacking specialized structural prompts and appropriate empathy.
projects.contribution: Authored model integration workflows in Python, created system instruction parameters, and connected Firebase authentication layers.
achievements: Specialized prompt engineering template to deliver grounded, empathetic guidance.
Secure customer sign-up and stateful profile authentication managed by Firebase.
Persistent conversation timelines securely synchronized with Cloud Firestore.

researchPublications.title: The Human Microbiome: Impact of the Gut Microbiome on Human Immunity and Metabolism
researchPublications.journal: International Journal of Progressive Research in Engineering Management and Science (IJPREMS)
researchPublications.description: An in-depth research study analyzing the complex, symbiotic relationship of the human gut microbiome and its critical impact on host immune responses, metabolic regulation, and general health maintenance. The manuscript compiles modern clinical literature on microbial ecosystems and outlines prospective therapeutic avenues for treating chronic metabolic disorders.

responsibilities: Developed a high-performance AI-based Fake URL Detection system using machine learning and deep learning strategies.
Collected, compiled, and preprocessed high-dimensional URL data to analyze phishing and legitimate website records.
Extracted key lexical and domain-based features to optimize URL representation models.
Built and trained robust classification models in PyTorch and TensorFlow, incorporating CNNs, RNNs, and LSTMs.
Applied Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using LoRA and QLoRA to configure advanced transformer architectures.
achievements: Evaluated classification scores across standard metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score to compare multiple experimental classifiers.
Optimized detection rates through iterative feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning, and seamless collaboration.

leadershipResponsibilities.description: Selected to lead Google developer student initiatives and cloud learning circles on campus. Coordinated developer bootcamps, structured student coding sessions, and introduced peers to Google's engineering toolchains.

Appointed as AWS Campus Builder to evangelize cloud solutions, containerization, and machine learning setups. Created instructional guides and shared cloud computing practices, supporting academic projects utilizing AWS environments.

additionalInformation.description: Active contributor to cloud knowledge-sharing. Passionate about AI integration in security platforms.
